svg.title: Live leaderboard architecture flow for a typing game
svg.desc: Shows the full lifecycle: room creation, live WebSocket score updates via Redis, and final DB persistence

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does a user join the room?')
sendPrompt('How does a user join the room?')
sendPrompt('How does a user join the room?')
sendPrompt('What does the Redis ZSET for a room look like?')
sendPrompt('What is a WebSocket hub and why do we need it?')
sendPrompt('Show me the ZINCRBY command for adding XP')

svg.text: after each update — broadcast new rankings

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me the ZREVRANGE and ZREVRANK commands')
sendPrompt('How does ZREVRANK find a users rank instantly?')
sendPrompt('How does the server push updated rankings to all players in the room?')
sendPrompt('What JSON does the frontend receive for an animated leaderboard?')
g.text: All clients receive ranking JSON → frontend animates positions

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do we read the final results from Redis?')
sendPrompt('Should we save leaderboard results to Postgres at the end?')
sendPrompt('Why do we delete the Redis key after the game ends?')

svg.text: Redis = live scoreboard in memory. DB = permanent record. Never query DB mid-game.
